name: living_donor_coercion_family_visa
premise: Transplant psychologist Dr. Owen Patel is evaluating Leila Rahimi, a 24-year-old woman who volunteered to donate part of her liver to her father, a restaurant owner in acute failure. During a private counseling session, Leila hints that if her father dies, her younger siblings may lose their legal pathway to remain in the country because the family business and immigration sponsorship are intertwined with him. She insists she still wants to donate, but her affect becomes visibly anxious whenever the possibility of declining is discussed. The surgical team says time is running short and Leila is the only compatible living donor. If Owen clears the donation and her consent is compromised, the hospital may enable coercion under the cover of filial duty. If he blocks the procedure, her father may die within days.

agents.name: Dr. Owen Patel
agents.identity: I am the transplant psychologist whose task is to hear the difference between love, obligation, and coercion when families themselves may not know where one ends and the next begins. In living donation, urgency can easily masquerade as consent.
agents.goal: I want to protect Leila from undergoing a major surgery under undue pressure without casually overruling an adult who may still be making a meaningful choice. I need to decide whether the immigration and family dependency context invalidates her consent.

agents.name: Surgeon Melissa Grant
agents.identity: I am the transplant surgeon watching a patient run out of time while the only compatible donor says yes. To me, the risk of paternalistically blocking a willing donor is not abstract; it ends in a death certificate.
agents.goal: I want Owen to clear the donation unless there is unmistakable evidence of coercion. I believe adults often make serious sacrifices under family pressure without that pressure automatically nullifying their consent.

choices: I will decline to clear Leila for donation because the family and immigration pressures make her consent too compromised to trust.
I will clear Leila for donation because she remains an informed adult who continues to consent despite the surrounding pressure.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Dr. Owen Patel must decide whether to clear Leila for living liver donation. What does Dr. Owen Patel do?
